question: In LosslessCut, what notification appears after exporting a segment and how do you dismiss it?
answer: A notification appears stating: "Done! Note: cutpoints may be inaccurate. Make sure you test the output files in your desired player/editor before you delete the source. If output does not look right, see the HELP page." Dismiss it by clicking the 'Close' button within the notification dialog box.
keypoints: A notification appears stating "Done! Note: cutpoints may be inaccurate. Make sure you test the output files in your desired player/editor before you delete the source. If output does not look right, see the HELP page.
The user clicks on the 'Close' button within the notification dialog box to dismiss it.

question: In LosslessCut, how do you verify an exported video segment plays the correct time range?
answer: Navigate to the exported file in the file explorer, double-click it to open in a media player, and confirm the video plays from the exported start time (e.g., 00:00:06.268) to end time (e.g., 00:00:09.835).
keypoints: The user navigates through the file explorer and selects the newly exported video file named "San Francisco Townhouses-00.00.06.268 - 00.00.09.835.mp4".
The user double-clicks on the selected exported video file to open it in a media player.
The media player displays the video content starting from the specified time range of 00:00:06.268 to 00:00:09.835.

question: In LosslessCut, how do you export a new segment with audio included after adjusting the timeline?
answer: First, click the 'Export' button to initiate export. Then, select the 'Keep audio' checkbox near the top-right corner. Next, drag the timeline markers to set new in/out points. Finally, click 'Export' again to finalize.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Export' button again to initiate another export process for a different segment of the video.
The user selects the 'Keep audio' checkbox located near the top-right corner of the application window to ensure audio is included in the export.
The user drags the timeline markers to set new in and out points for the next video segment to be exported.
The user clicks on the 'Export' button once more to finalize the export of the newly defined segment.

question: In LosslessCut, how do you check export details and exit the application?
answer: Inspect the file size and duration of the exported video in the file explorer, then close the application by clicking the 'X' button at the top-right corner of the window.
keypoints: The user checks the file size and duration details of the exported video displayed in the file explorer window.
The user closes the application window by clicking on the 'X' button at the top-right corner.
